 {"id":"bd-2cgj","title":"Fix symbol request zone validation + tailscale tag conversion panic","description":"Audit fix: remove panic in ZoneTagMapping::zone_to_tag by returning Result; add zone_id consistency validation for SymbolRequest (header vs request, and meta vs request) to prevent cross-zone confusion.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"TurquoiseIsland","created_at":"2026-02-02T00:27:16.585637280Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T00:40:27.650964593Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T00:40:27.650893671Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2cqj","title":"[IDEA-04] Mesh-backed Cursor/Offset Store Helper","description":"# Mesh-backed Cursor/Offset Store Helper

## Goal
Provide a standard SDK helper for polling connectors to persist cursors/offsets as mesh state (not local disk), with lease-aware single-writer semantics.

## Background / Why
Telegram/Gmail-style polling requires durable offsets. FCP2 prohibits authoritative local disk state. A standard CursorStore prevents drift and double-polling and aligns with ConnectorState requirements.

## Scope
- Define cursor state schema and helper in SDK.
- Integrate with ConnectorState/lease mechanics (singleton_writer).

## Deliverables
- `CursorStore` trait + default implementation using mesh objects.
- Helper APIs for commit, snapshot, and recovery.
- Docs + example for polling connectors.

## Tests
- Unit tests for monotonic sequence and recovery.
- Integration test with lease fencing and failover.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Polling connectors can persist offsets without bespoke state code.
- Cursor state survives restarts and prevents double-processing.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:31:21.495795618Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T23:39:56.715931697Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T23:39:56.715795534Z","close_reason":"CursorStore implementation + tests already satisfy acceptance criteria","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2cqj","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:32:26.718322142Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2cqj","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.33","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:32:09.509341347Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2cqj","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.34","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:32:18.483818103Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2ctr","title":"[CAP] Analyzer + recommendation engine","description":"# [CAP] Analyzer + recommendation engine

## Goal
Compute minimal capability sets and flag unused/risky capabilities.

## Deliverables
- Recommendation engine with explainable rules:
  - Unused capability detection (time-windowed).
  - Risk budget summaries per zone.
  - Suggested removals with rationale.
- Exportable report model (JSON).

## Tests
- Unit tests for deterministic outputs.
- Property tests for stability under input ordering.

## Logging Requirements
- Structured JSON logs for recommendation tests:
  - {test, zone_id, suggestions_count, result, duration_ms}.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Recommendations are deterministic and explainable.
- Reports include rationale and safe defaults.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"SilentDeer","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:07:28.033369599Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T18:59:11.805424525Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T18:59:11.805343544Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ctr","depends_on_id":"bd-1yug","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:08:18.916539175Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ctr","depends_on_id":"bd-3iac","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:07:28.047164733Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2f42","title":"[POL] PolicyBundle schema + signatures","description":"# [POL] PolicyBundle schema + signatures

## Goal
Define the PolicyBundle object schema and signature requirements with deterministic encoding.

## Deliverables
- PolicyBundle schema IDs + versioning.
- Canonical encoding rules (CBOR/JSON) and hash algorithm selection.
- Signature binding to zone_id, policy_seq, and bundle hash.
- Compatibility rules for policy engine (accepted fields, defaults).

## Design Notes
- Bundles are immutable; updates create new bundle objects.
- Signatures must chain to owner key or authorized policy signer.
- Fail closed on signature or schema errors.

## Tests
- Golden vectors for encoding + verification.
- Determinism tests (stable hash across OS/toolchains).

## Logging Requirements
- Structured JSON logs for schema/verification tests:
  - {test, bundle_id, result, reason_code?, duration_ms}.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Policy bundles validate deterministically and fail closed on signature errors.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"HazyForge","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:00:40.871047448Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T05:53:01.464552936Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2f42","depends_on_id":"bd-24bc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:00:40.877647702Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2f42","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.26","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:01:58.498510642Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-2f42","title":"[POL] PolicyBundle schema + signatures","description":"# [POL] PolicyBundle schema + signatures

## Goal
Define the PolicyBundle object schema and signature requirements with deterministic encoding.

## Deliverables
- PolicyBundle schema IDs + versioning.
- Canonical encoding rules (CBOR/JSON) and hash algorithm selection.
- Signature binding to zone_id, policy_seq, and bundle hash.
- Compatibility rules for policy engine (accepted fields, defaults).

## Design Notes
- Bundles are immutable; updates create new bundle objects.
- Signatures must chain to owner key or authorized policy signer.
- Fail closed on signature or schema errors.

## Tests
- Golden vectors for encoding + verification.
- Determinism tests (stable hash across OS/toolchains).

## Logging Requirements
- Structured JSON logs for schema/verification tests:
  - {test, bundle_id, result, reason_code?, duration_ms}.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Policy bundles validate deterministically and fail closed on signature errors.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"OliveCastle","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:00:40.871047448Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T03:02:39.332520067Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2f42","depends_on_id":"bd-24bc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:00:40.877647702Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2f42","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.26","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:01:58.498510642Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2f72","title":"[SC] CLI: verify/report/install gating","description":"# [SC] CLI: verify/report/install gating

## Goal
Expose supply-chain verification workflows to operators and CI via fcp-cli.

## Commands
- `fcp connector verify <bundle|manifest|binary>`
  - Validates attestations/SBOMs and emits evidence bundle (JSON).
- `fcp connector install --verify`
  - Enforces verification and blocks on failure.
- `fcp connector report --supply-chain`
  - Summarizes provenance, SBOM, trust roots, and policy decisions.

## Output Requirements
- Human and JSON output formats.
- Stable, machine-parseable JSON (for CI) with error codes.
- Exit codes: 0 = verified, non-zero = failed (distinct codes for policy vs signature vs mismatch).

## Tests
- Unit tests for CLI parsing and formatting (human + JSON snapshots).
- Integration tests with test host + signed/unsigned fixtures.
- Structured JSON logs for each verification step and decision.

## Acceptance Criteria
- CLI fails closed on verification errors; errors are actionable.
- JSON output is deterministic and suitable for CI gating.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-01T02:59:22.928136987Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T05:23:23.951758550Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2f72","depends_on_id":"bd-33no","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T02:59:22.936334621Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2f72","depends_on_id":"bd-3rgx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:00:00.083456501Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2f72","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.22","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:00:03.168102736Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2ff5","title":"TEST-SANDBOX: Sandbox + egress enforcement integration","description":"# TEST-SANDBOX: Sandbox + egress enforcement integration

## Goal
Verify real sandbox enforcement (seccomp/seatbelt/AppContainer/WASI) and NetworkConstraints in integration tests.

## Scope
- fcp-sandbox OS profiles (native + WASI).
- Egress proxy policy: localhost/private/tailnet deny defaults, SNI enforcement, SPKI pinning.

## Constraints
- Use actual sandbox backends where supported; skip with explicit reason on unsupported platforms.
- No mocked network policy checks.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests prove deny-by-default egress and allowlist behavior.
- Tests emit structured logs showing policy decisions and blocked destinations.

 {"id":"bd-2g5v","title":"[IDEA-19.1] Implement log secret/PII scanner","description":"## What
Implement a JSONL scanner with regex-based secret detection + allowlist support.

## How
- Parse each line into JSON or treat as text fallback.
- Maintain rule set for tokens (JWT, AWS keys, OAuth bearer, OpenAI/Anthropic patterns).
- Provide allowlist annotations for test fixtures.

## Success Criteria
- Scanner returns structured findings with line numbers and rule IDs.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- Each finding includes rule_id, line number, and redacted preview.
- Scanner supports `--json` output for CI integration.
